title: tmuxcc client → server-proxy messages
description: All control-plane messages sent by the client to the server-proxy. The client uses the server-proxy wire to discover sessions, claim session-proxy endpoints, and issue lifecycle commands.

SessionClaimCommand.description: Claim or obtain the session-proxy endpoint for a named session. Creates the session if it does not exist. Per-name atomic: concurrent claims are serialized. The created flag in the response indicates whether this claim minted the tmux session.

SessionCreateCommand.description: Create a brand-new session with the given name. Fails (session.name-taken) if a session with that name already exists.

SessionDestroyCommand.description: Destroy an existing session and reap its session-proxy.

PaneAttachCommand.description: Attach a new client to a specific pane within a session (tc-7xv.36). Returns the same session-proxy endpoint as session.claim; paneId is echoed back as an attach intent hint. The server-proxy does NOT validate pane existence — that is a session-proxy concern.

ServerProxyInfoCommand.description: Read-only server-proxy diagnostics snapshot (tc-k6v). No side-effects. Older server-proxies respond with protocol.unknown-message.

ServerProxyCommand.description: Discriminated union of all server-proxy commands a client may issue. Narrow on `kind`.

ServerProxyCommandRequestMessage.description: Client issues a server-proxy-level command. direction: client→server-proxy.

correlationId.description: Client-generated opaque string, echoed in the matching response.

ServerProxyClientMessage.description: Discriminated union of all messages the client sends to the server-proxy. Note: ClientCapabilitiesMessage is shared between both wires.
